ucs.title: [LLM] Akira (Howling Scorpius) signature WMI shadow-copy wipe via PowerShell
ucs.description: Hunts the exact PowerShell WMI command Akira operators run pre-encryption to nuke Volume Shadow Copies — `Get-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy | Remove-WmiObject` — documented verbatim in CISA AA24-109A and the Sophos/Mandiant/Microsoft Howling Scorpius reports as Akira tradecraft. This pattern is rarer and more specific than vssadmin.exe deletes and is highly indicative of Akira/PUNK SPIDER ransomware staging.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND Processes.process="*Win32_Shadowcopy*" AND (Processes.process="*Remove-WmiObject*" OR Processes.process="*Remove-CimInstance*" OR Processes.process="*Get-WmiObject*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(user,"(?i)\\$$") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// Akira ransomware — WMI/CIM shadow-copy wipe via PowerShell (CISA AA24-109A)
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has "Win32_Shadowcopy"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Remove-WmiObject","Remove-CimInstance","Get-WmiObject","Get-CimInstance",".Delete()")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          IsRemote = InitiatingProcessTokenElevation,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Akira ransomware — WMI/CIM shadow-copy wipe via PowerShell (CISA AA24-109A)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "powershell.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "pwsh.exe"
| where CommandLine has "Win32_Shadowcopy"
| where CommandLine has_any ("Remove-WmiObject","Remove-CimInstance","Get-WmiObject","Get-CimInstance",".Delete()")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Akira Ransomware Shadow Copy Deletion via PowerShell WMI
id: 7f1d6a2e-9b21-4f4a-8a4c-akira-vss-wmi
status: stable
description: Detects the Akira/Howling Scorpius signature command for VSS deletion via Get-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy piped to Remove-WmiObject (CISA AA24-109A).
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-109a
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1047
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
  selection_obj:
    CommandLine|contains: 'Win32_Shadowcopy'
  selection_verb:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'Remove-WmiObject'
      - 'Remove-CimInstance'
      - 'Get-WmiObject'
      - 'Get-CimInstance'
      - '.Delete()'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Backup software lifecycle scripts (rare in user-space; verify by parent process and signing)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Akira ransomware (aka GOLD SAHARA / PUNK SPIDER / Howling Scorpius) is documented by CISA, FBI and Sophos as deploying this exact PowerShell+WMI one-liner before encryption — distinct from the more common `vssadmin delete shadows` used by other crews. The combination of `Win32_Shadowcopy` and a CIM/WMI removal verb is a near-deterministic Akira fingerprint with negligible benign use-case in user/a
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Akira post-compromise rclone/WinSCP exfil to Mega.nz / cloud sync
ucs.description: Hunts Akira's characteristic data-exfil chain — rclone.exe (often renamed) or WinSCP staging archives created by 7z/WinRAR and pushed to Mega.nz, ngrok, or transfer.sh — that operators run after RDP/SMB lateral movement and before deploying the ransomware payload (T1567.002 over TLS, paired with T1219 AnyDesk/RustDesk for persistence). This sequence appears in nearly every Akira post-incident report (Arctic Wolf, Sophos, Mandiant, CISA AA24-109A).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as proc values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process="*rclone*" OR Processes.process="*WinSCP*" OR Processes.original_file_name="rclone.exe") AND (Processes.process="*mega*" OR Processes.process="*--config*" OR Processes.process="*copy *" OR Processes.process="*--transfers*" OR Processes.process="*remote:*" OR Processes.process="*ngrok*" OR Processes.process="*transfer.sh*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(user,"(?i)\\$$") | join type=outer host [| tstats summariesonly=t count as net_bytes values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as ports from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest="*mega.nz" OR All_Traffic.dest="*mega.co.nz" OR All_Traffic.dest="*ngrok.io" OR All_Traffic.dest="*transfer.sh" OR All_Traffic.dest="*anydesk.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*rustdesk.com") by All_Traffic.src | rename All_Traffic.src as host] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// Akira exfil chain: rclone/WinSCP -> Mega/ngrok/transfer.sh, optionally paired with AnyDesk/RustDesk RMM
let WindowMin = 30m;
let ExfilProcs = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where (FileName in~ ("rclone.exe","winscp.exe","winscp.com")
             or InitiatingProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName =~ "rclone.exe"
             or ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName =~ "rclone.exe")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("copy ","sync ","--config","--transfers","--multi-thread-streams","mega:","mega.nz","remote:","ngrok","transfer.sh","--no-check-certificate","-q --ignore-existing")
    | project ProcTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ExfilTool = FileName, ExfilCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let ExfilNet = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where RemoteUrl has_any ("mega.nz","mega.co.nz","userstorage.mega.co.nz","ngrok.io","transfer.sh","anonfiles.com","gofile.io","anydesk.com","rustdesk.com")
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, NetProc = InitiatingProcessFileName;
ExfilProcs
| join kind=inner ExfilNet on DeviceId
| where NetTime between (ProcTime - WindowMin .. ProcTime + WindowMin)
| project ProcTime, NetTime, DeviceName, AccountName, ExfilTool, ExfilCmd,
          Parent, ParentCmd, NetProc, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Akira exfil chain: rclone/WinSCP -> Mega/ngrok/transfer.sh on the same host within 30m
let WindowMin = 30m;
let ExfilProcs = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where NewProcessName has_any ("\\rclone.exe","\\winscp.exe","\\winscp.com")
    | where CommandLine has_any ("copy ","sync ","--config","--transfers","--multi-thread-streams","mega:","mega.nz","remote:","ngrok","transfer.sh")
    | project ProcTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ExfilCmd = CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
let ExfilNet = CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where DestinationHostName has_any ("mega.nz","mega.co.nz","userstorage.mega.co.nz","ngrok.io","transfer.sh","anonfiles.com","gofile.io","anydesk.com","rustdesk.com")
         or RequestURL has_any ("mega.nz","mega.co.nz","ngrok.io","transfer.sh","anydesk.com","rustdesk.com")
    | project NetTime = TimeGenerated, SrcHost = SourceHostName, DestinationHostName, RequestURL, DestinationIP;
ExfilProcs
| join kind=inner ExfilNet on $left.Computer == $right.SrcHost
| where NetTime between (ProcTime - WindowMin .. ProcTime + WindowMin)
| project ProcTime, NetTime, Computer, Account, ExfilCmd, ParentProcessName, DestinationHostName, RequestURL, DestinationIP
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.rationale: Akira's double-extortion playbook is consistently rclone-to-Mega (often the binary renamed `svchost.exe` or `winsystem.exe`) plus AnyDesk/RustDesk for hands-on-keyboard. The two-condition correlation (rclone-style cmdline AND egress to Mega/ngrok/transfer.sh on the same host within 30 min) is far tighter than single-table rclone or single-domain hunts and reflects the ordered TTP chain T1078 VPN a
